aliases: ポークカツカレーライス

japanese: ポークカツカレーライス

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for pork katsu curry rice; 'curry dish' (FOODON_00002809) is the closest defensible parent class. A more specific term for Japanese katsu curry is not available in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW (curry with meat and rice) is the closest defensible match for pork katsu curry rice. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for Japanese-style katsu curry specifically.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for pork katsu curry rice as a composite dish. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular codes for prepared mixed dishes of this type.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of pork katsu curry rice weighs approximately 400 g, comprising a portion of steamed white rice topped with a breaded deep-fried pork cutlet and Japanese curry sauce with vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Pork Katsu Curry Rice — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Pork Katsu Curry Rice (CR ID: F05100107). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pork loin, breaded and fried (NDB 35196) and Japanese curry sauce components, as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this dish; this is documented as an adapted value.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pork katsu curry rice as a composite dish in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate component: white jasmine rice (GI ~64–72, University of Sydney GI Database) combined with the moderating effect of the curry sauce (fat, protein, and fibre slow gastric emptying). A conservative mid-range estimate of 65 is applied. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × carbohydrate per serving (63.4 g from 400 g portion × 15.84 g/100g) / 100 = 41.2 g GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 361 mg/100 g; estimated 1,444 mg per standard 400 g serving, representing 63% of the WHO daily sodium limit of 2,300 mg
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a standard serve, request low-sodium curry roux, and avoid adding soy sauce or pickled condiments, as a single full serving may contribute over 60% of the recommended daily sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white rice base (estimated GI 65), low dietary fibre (0.84 g/100 g), and curry roux containing starch thickeners and added sugar elevate postprandial blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute white rice with brown rice or cauliflower rice, request curry sauce without added sugar or starch thickeners, and limit the portion to half a standard serve to reduce glycaemic load from an estimated 41 g to approximately 20 g per meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying in vegetable oil contributes saturated fat (estimated 1.8 g/100 g) and potential trans fat from repeated frying; high sodium further elevates cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose baked or air-fried pork cutlet alternatives, request reduced-sodium curry sauce, and limit consumption to occasional servings given the combined saturated fat and sodium burden of a standard 400 g plate.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; pork arachidonic acid drives pro-inflammatory cytokine production; potato in curry sauce is a nightshade that may trigger individual flares
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should request baked or grilled pork instead of deep-fried katsu, confirm whether the curry sauce contains potato or tomato paste (nightshades), and avoid this dish during active flare periods.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork Katsu Curry Rice (ポークカツカレーライス; 猪排咖喱饭) is a Japanese comfort dish consisting of a breaded, deep-fried pork loin cutlet (tonkatsu) served on a bed of steamed white rice and smothered in a thick, mildly spiced Japanese curry sauce. The curry sauce is typically made from a commercial roux block containing wheat flour, vegetable oil, curry spices, and starch thickeners, simmered with onion, potato, and carrot. The pork cutlet is coated in panko breadcrumbs and deep-fried in vegetable oil, yielding a crispy golden exterior with a juicy, tender interior. The dish has a rich, savoury, mildly sweet flavour profile with umami depth from the pork and curry roux. Per 100 g, the dish provides 148 kcal, 6.28 g protein, 6.67 g fat, and 15.84 g carbohydrate, with sodium at 361 mg — a clinically significant level for hypertension management. Dietary fibre is low at 0.84 g/100 g, and the white rice base creates a medium-to-high glycaemic load. The dish is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis without significant modification. It is widely served in Japanese restaurants, food courts, and casual dining establishments across Singapore and Southeast Asia.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID: F05100107) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as HPB does not report this separately; this is the only adapted value. GI is an analogous estimate based on white rice GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. All other values are exact from the primary approved source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (F05100107) and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for an equivalent composite Japanese curry rice dish as of 2026. Saturated fat (1.8 g/100 g) is an adapted estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for breaded fried pork loin and Japanese curry sauce components. The input record incorrectly classified this dish under 'mamak' cuisine; this has been corrected to 'Japanese' based on the dish's established culinary origin. The 'low_calorie' dietary tag from the input has been removed as 148 kcal/100 g for a mixed rice dish does not meet standard low-calorie thresholds. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on white jasmine rice GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for pork katsu curry rice exists in the approved GI database.
